name: Multi-Agent Swarm Orchestration
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for swarm supervisor routing, specialist agent reasoning, and dynamic delegation

prod.note: ~3M tokens/day, GPT-4o for specialists + GPT-4o-mini for routing

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, guaranteed latency across swarm agents

services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Swarm supervisor + dynamic specialist agent containers with Dapr sidecars

prod.note: 1 vCPU, 2GB per agent, 5-8 agent containers, min 2 replicas

enterprise.note: 2 vCPU, 4GB per agent, 10+ agents, multi-region, 3+ replicas

services.name: Azure Service Bus
services.purpose: Reliable async messaging for agent delegation, task queues, and dead-letter handling

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Swarm state coordination, agent task ledger, conversation memory, conflict resolution logs

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Agent credentials, API keys, tool-access tokens per specialist

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Distributed tracing across swarm agents, delegation paths, token spend per agent

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging for swarm containers — KQL queries, conflict resolution audits

services.name: Content Safety
services.purpose: Per-agent output moderation — validates each specialist's response before delegation chain continues

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for the supervisor routing layer — saves 80% on delegation decisions
Implement agent result caching in Cosmos DB to avoid redundant specialist invocations
Scale idle specialist agents to zero via Container Apps Consumption plan
Use Service Bus sessions for ordered task processing — avoids duplicate work
Set per-agent token budgets to cap runaway multi-turn costs
Batch low-priority agent tasks into off-peak queues for reduced compute spend
